title: Megalastrum oreocharis
intro: Megalastrum oreocharis, also known as the "oreocharis megalastrum," is a species of fern in the family Lomariopsidaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been found to possess several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. Bioactive constituents of Megalastrum oreocharis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Megalastrum oreocharis is classified within the family Lomariopsidaceae, which is a family of ferns that are part of the larger group known as Polypodiophyta. This classification is based on the plant's unique characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other fern species. The genus Megalastrum is a relatively small group of ferns that are primarily found in the Americas. Within the family Lomariopsidaceae, Megalastrum oreocharis is one of several species that share similar traits. Further research may be needed to confirm the exact classification of Megalastrum oreocharis within the broader context of plant taxonomy.
